title: Investigation: The Irish Republican Socialist Party (NI)
text:
Subject: The Irish Republican Socialist Party (NI)

Investigated for: Failure to deliver the 2023 statement of accounts by the due date

Decision: One offence was found A £350 Variable Monetary Penalty was imposed, which was paid on 13 February 2026

Summary of reasons: A sanction was considered appropriate and proportionate in this case in line with our Enforcement Policy
subject: The Irish Republican Socialist Party (NI)
investigated_for: Failure to deliver the 2023 statement of accounts by the due date
decision_text: One offence was found A £350 Variable Monetary Penalty was imposed, which was paid on 13 February 2026
reasoning: A sanction was considered appropriate and proportionate in this case in line with our Enforcement Policy

title: Investigation: Best for Britain
text:
Subject: Best for Britain

Investigated for: Failure to return an impermissible donation of £50,000 within 30 days of receipt

Decision: Two offences were found A £900 Variable Monetary Penalty imposed, which was paid on 9 December

Summary of reasons: The £50,000 impermissible donation was voluntarily returned by the campaigner, although after the 30-day deadline. A sanction was considered appropriate and proportionate in this case in line with our Enforcement Policy

title: Investigation: Propel (GB)
text:
Subject: Propel (GB)

Investigated for: The late reporting of two non-cash donations

Decision: No determination of offence made

Summary of reasons: We decided to close the investigation on the basis that it was not proportionate to continue, as this was a particularly complex and nuanced matter for the Party which could not be easily addressed by our published advice and guidance. We considered these facts and made no determination of any offences as the public register of donations was brought up to date and transparency was upheld for voters.
subject: Propel (GB)
investigated_for: The late reporting of two non-cash donations
reasoning: We decided to close the investigation on the basis that it was not proportionate to continue, as this was a particularly complex and nuanced matter for the Party which could not be easily addressed by our published advice and guidance. We considered these facts and made no determination of any offences as the public register of donations was brought up to date and transparency was upheld for voters.

title: Investigation: Local First Party (GB)
text:
Subject: Local First Party (GB)

Investigated for: Failure to deliver the 2023 statement of accounts by the due date

Decision: No offence. Closed without further action.

Summary of reasons: A reasonable excuse for the non-submission was provided. The offence can only be found where there is no reasonable excuse and therefore no offence was committed.
subject: Local First Party (GB)
investigated_for: Failure to deliver the 2023 statement of accounts by the due date
decision_text: No offence. Closed without further action.
reasoning: A reasonable excuse for the non-submission was provided. The offence can only be found where there is no reasonable excuse and therefore no offence was committed.

title: Investigation: Liberal Democrats (GB)
text:
Subject: Liberal Democrats (GB)

Investigated for: The late reporting of five donations

Decision: No determination of offence

Summary of reasons: The Commission decided that in all circumstances of the case it was not proportionate to continue the investigation. It was specifically noted that four of the delayed reports related to a misunderstanding of the aggregation rules, and that the aggregation guidance had recently been updated. The age of the fifth late reported donation was taken into consideration as well as changes to party officials. No determination of offence was therefore made, and the case was closed with no further action.

investigated_for: The late reporting of five donations

No determination of offence was therefore made, and the case was closed with no further action.

title: Investigation: True & Fair Party (GB) (de-registered political party)
text:
Subject: True & Fair Party (GB) (de-registered political party)

Investigated for: Failure to return an impermissible donation of £50,000 within 30 days of receipt

Decision: One offence found but no sanction imposed

Summary of reasons: The £50,000 impermissible donation was voluntarily returned by the party, although after the 30-day deadline, and the party has since de-registered with the Commission. We decided not to impose a sanction, as it was neither proportionate nor in the public interest to do so in the circumstances of the case.

title: Investigation: Kingston Independent Residents Group (GB)
text:
Subject: Kingston Independent Residents Group (GB)

Investigated for: Late delivery of Statement Of Accounts (SOA) for 2023

Decision: No determination of offence

Summary of reasons: The Commission decided to discontinue the investigation as a reasonable excuse for the delay was provided. No determination of offence was therefore made, and the case was closed with no further action.

investigated_for: Late delivery of Statement Of Accounts (SOA) for 2023
reasoning: The Commission decided to discontinue the investigation as a reasonable excuse for the delay was provided. No determination of offence was therefore made, and the case was closed with no further action.

title: Investigation: Northern Independence Party (GB) (now de-registered)
text:
Subject: Northern Independence Party (GB) (now de-registered)

Investigated for: Late delivery of Statement Of Accounts (SOA) for 2023

Decision: No determination of offence

Summary of reasons: The Commission decided that it would not be proportionate to continue with the investigation as the Party provided the outstanding SOA to the Commission, thus restoring transparency in political finance, and was no longer registered as a political party. No determination of offence was therefore made, and the case was closed with no further action.

No determination of offence was therefore made, and the case was closed with no further action.

title: Investigation: Labour Campaign For Electoral Reform (LCER)
text:
Subject: Labour Campaign For Electoral Reform (LCER)

Investigated for: Failing to report recordable donations on time

Decision: No further action

Summary of reasons: Sanctions were not considered appropriate given the mitigation provided by LCER. This decision was in line with our Enforcement Policy.

investigated_for: Failing to report recordable donations on time
reasoning: Sanctions were not considered appropriate given the mitigation provided by LCER. This decision was in line with our Enforcement Policy.

title: Investigation: Irish Republican Socialist Party (NI)
text:
Subject: Irish Republican Socialist Party (NI)

Investigated for: Failing to keep accounting records that are sufficient to show and explain the Party’s transactions

Decision: One contravention found A £250 Variable Monetary Penalty was imposed, which is due to be paid by on 3 December 2024

Summary of reasons: Sanctions were considered appropriate and proportionate in this case in line with our Enforcement Policy

title: Investigation: Barnes and Richmond Labour Club and Institute (BRLCI)
text:
Subject: Barnes and Richmond Labour Club and Institute (BRLCI)

Investigated for: Failing to deliver on time a notification of political contributions made in excess of £25,000, and three reports of gifts received

Decision: Four offences were found A £500 Variable Monetary Penalty was imposed, which is due to be paid by 1 November 2024

Summary of reasons: Sanctions were considered appropriate and proportionate in this case in line with our Enforcement Policy

title: Investigation: Richmond Park Constituency Labour Party (CLP)
text:
Subject: Richmond Park Constituency Labour Party (CLP)

Investigated for: Failing to deliver its audited statement of accounts for 2022 on time

Decision: One offence found for failing to deliver its audited statement of accounts for 2022 on time. A Variable Monetary Penalty of £1,000 was imposed, which was paid on 26 July.

Summary of reasons: Sanctions were considered appropriate and proportionate in this case in line with our Enforcement Policy.
